ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated radish cake code. A0CKP (dim sum and Chinese savoury pastries) is the closest defensible parent category.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of crispy spicy radish cake at a dim sum restaurant consists of approximately 3 to 4 pan-fried pieces, weighing around 150 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: Lo Bak Go (Radish Cake) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and HPB Singapore Foodo compositional data for pan-fried radish cake
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'Crispy Spicy Radish Cake' exists in a single approved source. Values are adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo data for pan-fried radish cake (lo bak go) and cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central entries for rice flour-based dim sum preparations. The spicy seasoning (chilli, garlic) contributes negligible macronutrient change. Values represent a pan-fried preparation with moderate oil absorption.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of rice flour-based steamed and pan-fried dim sum products — analogous estimate based on rice flour cake GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for crispy spicy radish cake. GI estimated at 55 based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for rice flour-based steamed cakes and pan-fried dim sum products (GI range 50–60). Pan-frying with oil and the presence of dietary fibre from daikon radish moderates the glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as: (55 × 23.5 × 1.50) / 100 = 19.4 g per 150 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~380 mg per 100 g; ~570 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of crispy spicy radish cake due to its high sodium content from soy sauce and seasoning, with a single 150 g serving providing approximately 570 mg sodium — nearly 25% of the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load with low-to-moderate GI (GI ~55)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Crispy spicy radish cake has a GI of approximately 55 and a glycemic load of ~19 per 150 g serving, placing it in the moderate glycemic load range; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume it in controlled portions and pair it with non-starchy vegetables or protein to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate fat content with pan-frying oil and elevated sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The pan-fried preparation of this radish cake contributes approximately 7.2 g fat per 100 g, predominantly unsaturated, but the high sodium content (~570 mg per serving) and frequent dim sum consumption patterns may cumulatively elevate cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size and frequency.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Crispy Spicy Radish Cake (香辣萝卜糕, lo bak go) is a popular Cantonese dim sum dish widely served in Singapore and Hong Kong. It is made from a batter of grated white radish (daikon) and rice flour, seasoned with soy sauce, sesame oil, white pepper, garlic, and chilli, then steamed into a firm cake and subsequently pan-fried until the exterior is golden and crispy while the interior remains soft and slightly chewy. The spicy variant incorporates chilli and garlic for a pungent, savoury heat that distinguishes it from the classic plain version. Nutritionally, a 150 g serving provides approximately 252 kcal, 4.2 g protein, 10.8 g fat, and 35.3 g carbohydrate, with a glycemic index of approximately 55 (low-to-moderate). The dish is high in sodium (~570 mg per serving) due to soy sauce seasoning, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. It is suitable for vegetarians and vegans (when prepared without egg wash) and is halal-friendly. The daikon radish contributes dietary fibre and vitamin C. Clinically, portion control is advised for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes given the moderate glycemic load of ~19 per serving. This dish is commonly served as part of a dim sum spread at breakfast or brunch and is a staple at Cantonese restaurants across Singapore.

confidence_reason: No single approved source contains an exact entry for 'Crispy Spicy Radish Cake'. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo pan-fried radish cake data and USDA FoodData Central rice flour-based dim sum entries. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the adapted source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore Foodo (pan-fried radish cake / lo bak go) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy rice flour dim sum entries; no exact match for the spicy variant exists in a single approved source. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data for this preparation. 3. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 4. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 6. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 7. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the adapted source data. 8. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a plant-oil pan-fried product with no hydrogenated fats. 9. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this preparation contains no animal-derived ingredients in the standard vegan version. 10. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this is a plant-based preparation with no animal products. 11. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database based on rice flour-based steamed and pan-fried dim sum products; no direct GI measurement for this specific dish was found. GI category is 'low' per the ≤55 threshold. 12. The food_group has been reclassified from 'Desserts' (as in the input) to 'Snacks' as radish cake is a savoury dim sum snack, not a dessert. 13. Dietary flags reflect the standard vegan preparation; restaurant versions may include egg wash, which would change is_vegan and is_vegetarian flags.
